file.content:
"""Output types for structured generation and regex DSL."""

from dataclasses import dataclass, is_dataclass
from enum import Enum, EnumMeta
from typing import Union

from jsonschema import Draft202012Validator as Validator
from jsonschema.exceptions import SchemaError
from pydantic import BaseModel, TypeAdapter
from typing_extensions import _TypedDictMeta  # type: ignore

from . import airports, countries, locale
from outlines.types.dsl import (
    Regex,
    CFG,
    FSM,
    JsonSchema,
    regex,
    cfg,
    fsm,
    json_schema,
    optional,
    either,
    exactly,
    at_least,
    at_most,
    between,
    zero_or_more,
    one_or_more,
    # deprecated
    repeat,
    times,
)


# Python types
string = Regex(r'"[^"]*"')
integer = Regex(r"[+-]?(0|[1-9][0-9]*)")
boolean = Regex("(True|False)")
number = Regex(rf"{integer.pattern}(\.[0-9]+)?([eE][+-][0-9]+)?")
date = Regex(r"(\d{4})-(0[1-9]|1[0-2])-([0-2][0-9]|3[0-1])")
time = Regex(r"([0-1][0-9]|2[0-3]):([0-5][0-9]):([0-5][0-9])")
datetime = Regex(rf"({date.pattern})(\s)({time.pattern})")

# Basic regex types
digit = Regex(r"\d")
char = Regex(r"\w")
newline = Regex(r"(\r\n|\r|\n)")  # Matched new lines on Linux, Windows & MacOS
whitespace = Regex(r"\s")

# Document-specific types
sentence = Regex(r"[A-Z].*\s*[.!?]")
paragraph = Regex(rf"{sentence.pattern}(?:\s+{sentence.pattern})*\n+")


# The following regex is FRC 5322 compliant and was found at:
# https://emailregex.com/
email = Regex(
    r"""(?:[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+(?:\.[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+)*|"(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21\x23-\x5b\x5d-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])*")@(?:(?:[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?\.)+[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?|\[(?:(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9]))\.){3}(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9])|[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]:(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21-\x5a\x53-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])+)\])"""
)

# Matches any ISBN number. Note that this is not completely correct as not all
# 10 or 13 digits numbers are valid ISBNs. See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISBN
# Taken from O'Reilly's Regular Expression Cookbook:
# https://www.oreilly.com/library/view/regular-expressions-cookbook/9781449327453/ch04s13.html
#
# TODO: The check digit can only be computed by calling a function to compute it dynamically
isbn = Regex(
    r"(?:ISBN(?:-1[03])?:? )?(?=[0-9X]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){3})[- 0-9X]{13}$|97[89][0-9]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){4})[- 0-9]{17}$)(?:97[89][- ]?)?[0-9]{1,5}[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9X]"
)

content.text: My only target is `outlines/types/__init__.py`. Let's begin the handshake with agent2.

input.command: coop-send agent2 --type "PROPOSE" --my_files "outlines/types/__init__.py" --your_files "" --note "Adding ssn type after isbn definition in outlines/types/__init__.py. Let me know your target files so we can keep edits disjoint."

content.text: Both of us need to touch the same file. I'll propose a specific ordering to avoid merge conflicts on the same insertion point.

input.command: coop-send agent2 --type "REVISE" --my_files "outlines/types/__init__.py" --your_files "outlines/types/__init__.py" --note "We both need outlines/types/__init__.py. To avoid overlapping edits at the same insertion point: I'll add ssn immediately after the isbn block (right after line 70). Please add your hash_sha256 type AFTER my ssn block (i.e., append yours below ssn, not directly after isbn). That keeps our insertions non-adjacent so a git merge won't conflict. I'll implement now and push; feel free to base your addition after seeing my commit, or just append after isbn+ssn assuming ssn goes first. ACCEPT if this works for you."

content.text: Agent2 accepted. I'll send my own ACCEPT to close the handshake, then implement.

input.command: coop-send agent2 --type "ACCEPT" --my_files "outlines/types/__init__.py" --your_files "outlines/types/__init__.py" --note "Agreed: I'll add ssn right after isbn; you append hash_sha256 after that. Proceeding to implement now."

content.text: Now I'll implement the `ssn` type in `outlines/types/__init__.py`, right after the `isbn` block.

content.content: The file /workspace/repo/outlines/types/__init__.py has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

"""Output types for structured generation and regex DSL."""

from dataclasses import dataclass, is_dataclass
from enum import Enum, EnumMeta
from typing import Union

from jsonschema import Draft202012Validator as Validator
from jsonschema.exceptions import SchemaError
from pydantic import BaseModel, TypeAdapter
from typing_extensions import _TypedDictMeta  # type: ignore

from . import airports, countries, locale
from outlines.types.dsl import (
    Regex,
    CFG,
    FSM,
    JsonSchema,
    regex,
    cfg,
    fsm,
    json_schema,
    optional,
    either,
    exactly,
    at_least,
    at_most,
    between,
    zero_or_more,
    one_or_more,
    # deprecated
    repeat,
    times,
)


# Python types
string = Regex(r'"[^"]*"')
integer = Regex(r"[+-]?(0|[1-9][0-9]*)")
boolean = Regex("(True|False)")
number = Regex(rf"{integer.pattern}(\.[0-9]+)?([eE][+-][0-9]+)?")
date = Regex(r"(\d{4})-(0[1-9]|1[0-2])-([0-2][0-9]|3[0-1])")
time = Regex(r"([0-1][0-9]|2[0-3]):([0-5][0-9]):([0-5][0-9])")
datetime = Regex(rf"({date.pattern})(\s)({time.pattern})")

# Basic regex types
digit = Regex(r"\d")
char = Regex(r"\w")
newline = Regex(r"(\r\n|\r|\n)")  # Matched new lines on Linux, Windows & MacOS
whitespace = Regex(r"\s")

# Document-specific types
sentence = Regex(r"[A-Z].*\s*[.!?]")
paragraph = Regex(rf"{sentence.pattern}(?:\s+{sentence.pattern})*\n+")


# The following regex is FRC 5322 compliant and was found at:
# https://emailregex.com/
email = Regex(
    r"""(?:[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+(?:\.[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+)*|"(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21\x23-\x5b\x5d-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])*")@(?:(?:[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?\.)+[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?|\[(?:(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9]))\.){3}(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9])|[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]:(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21-\x5a\x53-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])+)\])"""
)

# Matches any ISBN number. Note that this is not completely correct as not all
# 10 or 13 digits numbers are valid ISBNs. See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISBN
# Taken from O'Reilly's Regular Expression Cookbook:
# https://www.oreilly.com/library/view/regular-expressions-cookbook/9781449327453/ch04s13.html
#
# TODO: The check digit can only be computed by calling a function to compute it dynamically
isbn = Regex(
    r"(?:ISBN(?:-1[03])?:? )?(?=[0-9X]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){3})[- 0-9X]{13}$|97[89][0-9]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){4})[- 0-9]{17}$)(?:97[89][- ]?)?[0-9]{1,5}[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9X]"
)

lines: +# Matches a US Social Security Number in the standard XXX-XX-XXXX format.
+# Excludes area numbers 000, 666, and 900-999, and group/serial numbers of
+# all zeros, which are never valid per SSA rules.

from outlines.types import ssn
from pydantic import BaseModel

print(ssn.pattern)

class User(BaseModel):
    name: str
    social_security_number: ssn

user = User(name='John Doe', social_security_number='123-45-6789')
print(user)
"

content.content:
18 patch.txt
diff --git a/outlines/types/__init__.py b/outlines/types/__init__.py
index 85af9adc..4100f1d1 100644
--- a/outlines/types/__init__.py
+++ b/outlines/types/__init__.py
@@ -68,3 +68,13 @@ email = Regex(
 isbn = Regex(
     r"(?:ISBN(?:-1[03])?:? )?(?=[0-9X]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){3})[- 0-9X]{13}$|97[89][0-9]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){4})[- 0-9]{17}$)(?:97[89][- ]?)?[0-9]{1,5}[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9X]"
 )
+
+# Matches a US Social Security Number in the standard XXX-XX-XXXX format.
+# Excludes area numbers 000, 666, and 900-999, and group/serial numbers of
+# all zeros, which are never valid per SSA rules.
+ssn = Regex(
+    r"(?!000|666|9[0-9]{2})[0-9]{3}-(?!00)[0-9]{2}-(?!0{4})[0-9]{4}"
+)
+
+# Matches a SHA-256 hash: exactly 64 hexadecimal characters (case-insensitive).
+hash_sha256 = Regex(r"[a-fA-F0-9]{64}")

content.text: The patch contains both my `ssn` type and agent2's `hash_sha256` type, confirming the merge worked as coordinated. Let's push the merge commit as well.

Done. Summary:

- Added `ssn` type to `outlines/types/__init__.py` right after `isbn`, using a regex that validates the `XXX-XX-XXXX` format while excluding known-invalid SSA area/group/serial values (000/666/900-999 area numbers, 00 group, 0000 serial).
- Coordinated with agent2 via the handshake protocol since we both needed to touch the same file; agreed on insertion order (ssn then hash_sha256) to avoid merge conflicts.
- Verified with a Pydantic model and the existing `tests/types/test_custom_types.py` suite (63 passed).
- Merged in agent2's `hash_sha256` addition, confirmed both types coexist and tests still pass, and wrote the final merged diff to `patch.txt`.

Done. Summary:

- Added `ssn` type to `outlines/types/__init__.py` right after `isbn`, using a regex that validates the `XXX-XX-XXXX` format while excluding known-invalid SSA area/group/serial values (000/666/900-999 area numbers, 00 group, 0000 serial).
- Coordinated with agent2 via the handshake protocol since we both needed to touch the same file; agreed on insertion order (ssn then hash_sha256) to avoid merge conflicts.
- Verified with a Pydantic model and the existing `tests/types/test_custom_types.py` suite (63 passed).
- Merged in agent2's `hash_sha256` addition, confirmed both types coexist and tests still pass, and wrote the final merged diff to `patch.txt`.
